text:
By Henry Moore

Team GB swimmer Adam Peaty has slammed the catering available at Paris 2024, claiming some athletes have found worms in their meals at the Olympic Village.

This comes after Peaty finished fourth in 4x100m medley relay on the final evening of swimming at the 2024 games.

In a bid to meet sustainability pledges organisers have aimed to make 60 per cent of all meals served meatless and a third plant-based, much to Peaty芒聙聶s disappointment.

芒聙聹The catering isn芒聙聶t good enough for the level the athletes are expected to perform. We need to give the best we possibly can,芒聙聺 the silver-medal-winning swimmer told the publication,芒聙聺he told the i.

芒聙聹Tokyo, the food was incredible, Rio was incredible. But this time around [芒聙娄] there wasn芒聙聶t enough protein options, long queues, waiting 30 minutes for food because there芒聙聶s no queuing system.

Read more:Olympic swimmer speaks out over 'lies' after being banned from athletes village for 'inappropriate conduct'

Read More:Olympic swimmer Luana Alonso removed from Athletes' Village over claims she created an 'inappropriate environment'

芒聙聹These [complaints] are for people to get better. And the organising committee, so we芒聙聶ll put these back to our team in full depth and detail.

芒聙聹But it芒聙聶s definitely been the best Games in terms of fans engaged with it. So you never gonna have a perfect Games.芒聙聺

A Paris 2024 spokesperson told the i: "We are listening to the athletes and take their feedback very seriously.

"Since the opening of the village, our partner Sodexo Live! has been working proactively to adapt supplies to the growing use of the Olympic Village restaurants, as well as to the actual consumption by athletes observed over the first few days.

"As a result, the quantities of certain products has been significantly increased and additional staff have been deployed to ensure the service runs smoothly."

Peaty slammed organisers for a lack of protein options and even claimed some athletes have found worms in their fish.

芒聙聹The narrative of sustainability has just been punished on the athletes. I want to meat, I need meat to perform and that芒聙聶s what I eat at home, so why should I change?芒聙聺 Peaty continued.

芒聙聹I like my fish and people are finding worms in the fish. It芒聙聶s just not good enough.

芒聙聹The standard, we芒聙聶re looking at the best of the best in the world, and we芒聙聶re feeding them not the best.

芒聙聹I just want people to get better at their roles and jobs. And I think that芒聙聶s what the athletes are the best sounding board for.芒聙聺

This isn芒聙聶t the first complaint Peaty, who is recovering from Covid, has expressed about Paris 2024.

Olympic organisers told LBC that no reports of worms in food have been made by Team GB athletes.

Yesterday, he hit out at the 芒聙聹bizarre芒聙聺 anti-doping rules that allowed two Chinese athletes who allegedly took banned substances to compete in the 4x100m medley relay, winning gold.

芒聙聹We have to be very careful not to paint a whole nation with one brush, firstly,芒聙聺 Peaty told LBC芒聙聶s Charlotte Lynch.

芒聙聹But also, that we kind of set a punishment, or set a barrier for those people who are cheating and it芒聙聶s very blatant.

芒聙聹Giving each nation their own responsibility and trust in that nation to test their own athletes 芒聙聯 it芒聙聶s bizarre.

芒聙聹We should have a full de-centralised testing agency that tests everyone fairly.

芒聙聹I want to play fair, I swim fair, I know how hard I worked to get here.芒聙聺
